reasoning: The FRED statistical tool estimates a 58% probability that the 1-year expected inflation rate will increase above the November 1 value (2.704) by early December, driven by a slight positive overall trend in the past year. However, the known baseline from the prompt text (2.745 in late October) is higher, making an increase more difficult to achieve. Looking at the last 13 months, only about 3-5 months have exceeded the 2.70-2.75 hurdle. Furthermore, recent momentum over the last few months shows a downward trajectory (dropping from ~2.79 in July to 2.70 in November). Balancing the tool's 0.58 statistical estimate with the higher reported baseline and recent cooling momentum, I set a probability of 0.53 for the short-term release (December 2025). For the longer-term resolution in February 2026, I pull the estimate closer to 0.50 (0.51) to reflect the increasing uncertainty over the forecast horizon.
